sections.paragraph:
κενοδοξέω to hold a vain or useless opinion, vainly imagine (s. next entry; Polyb. 12, 26c, 4; Dio Chrys. 21 [38], 29; 4 Macc 5:10; 8:24 κενοδοξήσωμεν; Philo, Mut. Nom. 226; Tat. 12, 1) MPol 10:1.

Polyb Polyb , III–II b.c.—List 5

Dio Chrys Dio Chrys , I–II a.d.—List 5

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Tat Tat , II a.d.—List 5

MPol MPol = Martyrdom of Polycarp; after II a.d.—List 1

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 538
